summary: Matched a large batch of fighter parts/model functions, primarily in src/melee/ft/ftparts.c, and refined related fighter/Kirby/Purin data structures and headers. The decomp.dev bot reported +7600 matched bytes and 24 new matches, including ftParts_80073830, ftParts_800739B8, ftParts_80073CA8, ftParts_80074194, ftParts_8007462C, ftParts_800753D4, ftParts_80075650, rotation helpers, and Kirby's ftKb_SpecialN_800EFB4C.
what_changed: Implemented many previously stubbed ftparts functions in ftparts.c, including matrix setup paths for PObj/JObj rendering, fighter part setup/traversal, DObj/TObj lookup, JObj insertion, model part attachment, DObj list collection, and fighter rotation accessors/mutators.

Updated ftparts.h prototypes from untyped void/UNK_T signatures to more specific signatures using HSD_PObj, HSD_Joint, HSD_JObj, Fighter_GObj, Fighter, DObjList, u32 pointers, and part_idx-style parameters.

Added src/melee/ft/dobjlist.h defining struct DObjList with u32 count and HSD_DObj** data, with a TODO noting it was split from ft/types.h to break a circular dependency with fighter-specific types.h headers.
Added src/melee/ft/ftparts.static.h for ft_jobj_scale, a .bss-like struct with Mtx mtx and has_z_scale bitfield.

Refined Fighter_804D6540 from int** to struct Fighter_804D6540_t** with nested struct Fighter_804D6540_x0_t entries containing x0/x1/x2/x3 bytes plus x4 count, enabling typed uses in ftParts_8007506C and ftParts_800753D4.
Updated Link and Young Link OnLoad code to pass Fighter_804D6540[fp->kind]->x0 instead of dereferencing Fighter_804D6540[fp->kind] as an int pointer.

Refined Fighter struct fields around fp+5AC and fp+5F4: introduced x5AC as u32 and replaced the previous x5F4/x5F5 union with x5F4_arr[2] entries containing s8 x0 and x1. Call sites in Common Escape and Samus were updated from fp->x5F5 to fp->x5F4_arr[0].x1.
Refined FighterBone layout with unions for flags8 and flagsC while retaining sizeof(struct FighterBone) == 0x10; added comments for lighting, texgen, and specular-related flags.

Updated Kirby hat data to use DObjList x14 instead of separate x14/unk_hsd_obj fields, renamed local Kirby hat access in ftKb_SpecialN_800EFB4C to KirbyHatStruct** hats and KirbyHatStruct* hat, loaded hat->hat_joint, and passed &hat->joint_num to ftParts_8007487C.
Updated Purin fighter vars to use DObjList x2240 instead of separate count/data fields, and adjusted allocation/free/model visibility calls to use x2240.data or x2240.count as appropriate.

Refined ftData and Kirby-related structs, including ftData->x8->x0 as u32 plus x4 padding, comments on x48_items, KirbyHatStruct::joint_num as u32, and Kirby_Unk::x4 as HSD_Joint* rather than HSD_Joint**.
smart_moves: Introduced a reusable DObjList type for paired count/data fields, which made Kirby and Purin accessory model code align with fighter dobj_list usage and clarified when callers should pass the count field versus the allocated data pointer.
Broke a header dependency cycle by moving DObjList out of ft/types.h into ft/dobjlist.h; the diff explicitly documents this as a temporary placement with a TODO.

Typed Fighter_804D6540 enough to replace pointer arithmetic/dereference guesses with named x0/x1/x2/x3 fields and an x4 count, directly supporting the matched implementation of ftParts_8007506C and ftParts_800753D4.

Used HSD accessor functions such as HSD_JObjGetChild, HSD_JObjGetNext, HSD_JObjGetParent, HSD_JObjGetFlags, HSD_JObjGetDObj, and HSD_JObjGetScaleX/Y/Z instead of raw macros or local duplicate getter inlines where possible.

Kept debug/assert strings inline in implemented functions such as OSReport("fighter parts num not match! player %d\n") and __assert("ftparts.c", ...), avoiding the earlier temporary static string variables once string reuse was no longer needed.

Converted formerly broad prototypes into typed ones in ftparts.h, which forced call sites in Kirby/Purin/Link/Young Link/Common/Samus to expose real field meaning and pointer levels needed for matching.
Marked unresolved structural uncertainty with TODOs instead of pretending the data is fully understood, notably for DObjList placement and Purin's ftData->x48_items similarity to KirbyHat handling.
decomp_lessons: Accessory or alternate-model systems often contain a count pointer and a DObj** data pointer side by side; representing them as DObjList can unlock cleaner calls to ftParts_8007487C, ftParts_80075650, ftParts_80075240, visibility toggles, allocation, and freeing.

When a field is used both as an aggregate and as individual bitfields, unions such as FighterBone.flags8 and FighterBone.flagsC can preserve layout while enabling initialization patterns like flags8 = 0 and flagsC = 0.
Global tables that start as int** may need small nested structs rather than casts. Here, Fighter_804D6540 needed an entry array pointer plus a count to support skipped/inserted fighter parts.
Replacing scalar aliases like x5F5 with indexed structs such as x5F4_arr[0].x1 can clarify array semantics and fix downstream comparisons without changing offsets.
Tree-walk matching in JObj hierarchies benefits from explicit left-child/next-sibling/parent traversal with special handling for JOBJ_INSTANCE and skipped parts from ftParts_8007506C.

For matched rendering code, keep GX/HSD matrix-load side effects and performance counters visible: GXSetCurrentMtx, GXLoadPosMtxImm, GXLoadNrmMtxImm, GXLoadTexMtxImm, HSD_PerfCurrentStat.nb_mtx_load, and HSD_PerfCountEnvelopeBlending all appear in the ftparts matches.

Fighter accessory initialization requires pairing default-class changes around object loads: ftParts_80074148 sets HSD_PObjSetDefaultClass(&ftPObj), ftParts_80074170 clears it, and ftParts_8007482C temporarily sets the ftIntpJObj class around HSD_JObjLoadJoint.
When matching old code that manipulates HSD object classes, changing material classes during DObj list construction via hsdChangeClass(mobj, &ftMObj) can be part of the required behavior.
naming_conventions: Use DObjList for structures with fields named count and data where data is HSD_DObj**.
Use part_idx for fighter part index parameters in rotation helpers and accessors instead of vague arg1.
Use rotate_x, rotate_y, and rotate_z for float rotation arguments in ftParts_8007592C, ftParts_80075AF0, and ftParts_80075CB4.
Use idx and val for simple indexed model-visibility state setters such as ftParts_80074A4C and ftParts_80074B0C.
Use Fighter_804D6540_t and Fighter_804D6540_x0_t for the newly typed Fighter_804D6540 global table entries until better semantic names are known.
Prefer KirbyHatStruct, hats, hat, hat_joint, and joint_num over temp_unk/temp_joint-style locals when the data is known to be Kirby hat data.
Keep unknown-but-structured fighter state as x5F4_arr[2].x0/x1 rather than flattening it into separate x5F4/x5F5/x5F6/x5F7 scalar aliases.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The PR matched 24 functions according to the bot report, with ftparts functions moving from 0.00% to 100.00% and Kirby's ftKb_SpecialN_800EFB4C moving from 79.43% to 100.00%.
Used PAD_STACK annotations in several functions to preserve stack shape, including ftParts_80074194, ftParts_80075240, ftParts_80075650, and Kirby/Purin-adjacent calls.
Preserved exact object traversal and matrix-mark logic in PObj setup functions using HSD_PObjGetMtxMark/HSD_PObjSetMtxMark and current JObj checks.
Modeled render flags explicitly around RENDER_SHADOW, JOBJ_LIGHTING, TEX_COORD_REFLECTION, and TEX_COORD_HILIGHT to drive normal/texture matrix loads.
Used casts where type knowledge is still imperfect but matching required pointer interpretation, such as ftParts_8007487C((void*) joint, ...) and ftParts_8007487C((void*) &items_shifted[1], ...).
Replaced temporary local scale getter inlines with HSD_JObjGetScaleX/Y/Z, likely relying on shared inline/string behavior now available in headers.

Matched data/string placement by removing many static string symbols from ftparts.c and using string literals at call sites; kept one temporary static string ftParts_803C0AE0 with a comment saying it only fills out the data section until ftParts_8007487C is matched.
Introduced ftparts.static.h for ft_jobj_scale so the .bss state used by matrix setup remained available without cluttering ftparts.c with broader placeholder declarations.

review_feedback.feedback: Matched code increased to 40.21% (+0.20%, +7600 bytes). The bot listed 24 new matches, mostly in main/melee/ft/ftparts plus ftKb_SpecialN_800EFB4C.
review_feedback.action_taken: PR was merged shortly after the bot report.

review_feedback.feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that match ftParts_8007487C and remove the temporary ftParts_803C0AE0 data-section filler.
Search for later cleanup of the TODO in ft/dobjlist.h about finding a better home for DObjList.
Search for later semantic names for Fighter_804D6540_t and Fighter_804D6540_x0_t fields x0/x1/x2/x3/x4.
Search for later cleanup of Purin ftPr_Init_8013C360 and ftData->x48_items after the TODO comparing it to ftKb_SpecialN_800EFB4C.
Check whether additional fighter-specific accessory code adopted DObjList count/data conventions after this PR.
